attributes.title: Content Guidelines
attributes.description:
Jardan Content Guidelines Summary

1. Colour Usage

  • Primary Palette: Define main brand colours and where they should be used.

  • Secondary Palette: Supporting colours for backgrounds, highlights, and subtle UI elements.

  • Contrast & Accessibility: Ensure WCAG-compliant contrast for readability.

  • Colour Pairings: Rules for which colours are to be used in which instances.

  • Do / Don’t Examples:

    • Do: Use primary colour for main CTAs.

    • Don’t: Overuse highlight colours or mix conflicting tones.

2. Image & Visual Style

  • Photography Style & Cropping

  • Composition Guidelines: Negative space, framing, orientation (landscape vs portrait).

  • File Quality: High-resolution, optimised for web, aspect ratios used.

3. Iconography

  • Iconography Style: Line vs filled, rounded vs sharp, consistent stroke weight.

  • Photography & Iconography Content Pairing: 2/3 examples of how these elements connect + rules.

4. Typography

  • Font Sizes: Font specs desktop vs. mobile.

  • Title & Headings Structure & Formatting : H1 (page title), H2 (section titles), padding rules.

  • Body Copy Structure & Formatting : Length guidance, padding rules.

  • Length: Length guidance for use-case for section.

  • Hierarchy Consistency: Never skip heading levels; maintain predictable structure

  • Type Case Rules: Sentence case vs title case

  • Do / Don’t Examples

5. Example Content Layout Principles

  • Whitespace: Rules for breathing room around sections.

  • Colour Use Cases: Which sections of the site use which colours e.g. Sustainability → green.

  • Modular Section Layout Use Cases: 3 examples of use cases of image, icon, text and rules for spacing → how this all fits together.

6. Spec Sheets & Downloadable Content

attributes.title: Change of Sizing PDP/PLP
attributes.description:
Hi @[{"type":"person","id":"373912","label":"Ellie White","avatar_url":"https://files.productive.io/people/avatars/18052edf-8dcd-45eb-9e65-68e81931cb1c/thumb/Screenshot_2025-05-23_at_8.33.41%E2%80%AFAM.png","attachment_url":null,"is_done":true}]

We are in the process of updating our sizing from our numerical 00-4 sizing to the universally recognized XXS-XL alpha sizing.

Our denim will also be updated to a global sizing standard of 22-32 which will also address the "in-between" sizing feedback we've been receiving by adding further sizes.

Sizing will transition on product from our Autumn collection on Feb 3rd.

Core will transition over the next 2 months with new size pips starting to come through already.

attributes.title: Phase 4: Roadmap: Investment, Sequencing and Readiness
attributes.description:
Purpose

Turn clarity into confident decision making without committing to premature build.

Activities

  • Translate strategy into realistic implementation pathways

  • Define sequencing aligned to March launches and near term priorities

  • Sense check effort against team capability and maturity


Output

  • Clear roadmap options with trade offs

  • Indicative scope, timing, and investment ranges

  • Risks and dependencies clearly articulated

attributes.title: SEO & AEO Blog Training Session
attributes.description:
Plan and deliver a 60–90 minute training session covering SEO and AEO best practices for blog content, including supporting materials and templates.

——-

Context

Overview
Decjuba has requested a training session for their marketing team to ensure blog content aligns with current SEO and AEO best practices.

Objective
Equip the Decjuba marketing team with practical guidelines to improve blog performance across organic search and answer-driven platforms (e.g. featured snippets, AI summaries).

Scope

  • 60–90 minute training session (virtual)

  • Focus on:

    • SEO fundamentals for blogs (keyword strategy, structure, internal linking, metadata)

    • AEO principles (intent matching, structured answers, FAQ schema, content formatting)

    • Blog structure templates for performance

    • Examples: what’s working vs. what’s not

    • Q&A session

Deliverables

  • Training session delivered

  • Slide deck shared post-session

  • SEO/AEO blog checklist template

  • Optional: recommended blog structure template

attributes.title: Information Architecture
attributes.description:
Overview

The Information Architecture (IA) output provides a clear and agreed structure for the new site. It maps how content and categories will be organised, ensuring customers can easily navigate and find what they need.

This work draws from analytics, audits, and stakeholder input to deliver a finalised sitemap and IA that will guide design, development, and content planning.

Further information

attributes.title: Incu - Online Optimisation
attributes.description:
Hey Ellie,

We’re currently working on an online optimisation project and I’d love to run you through the brief and key focus areas before we start raising tickets to have everything scoped properly.

At a high level, the objective is to create a more seamless conversion funnel and make it easier for customers to discover brands and products - primarily by levelling up our on-site search, filtering and merchandising experience.

This project will largely involve the Athos team, as we’ll be optimising both the design and functionality across:

Search & Filtering

  • Advanced filters

  • Size filters

  • Search bar optimisations

UX Improvements

  • Hover size availability for all products, plus ability to click/swipe through products at PLP level

  • Updated in-line banners

  • Updated badge design

  • “Shop by Fit” navigation functionality

  • Colour swatches

  • Improved size guides

We’re also exploring development of a few new homepage modules, including:

  • Product gallery module

  • Full-bleed multi-column module

  • New CTA capabilities so we can run multiple messages i.e. separate 'Mens' & 'Womens' buttons on one module 

For Incu Collection specifically, we’re considering creating a dedicated landing page / website tab to give the brand a more distinct experience separate from the main site - similar to how Aje structures Aje, Aje Studio and Aje Athletica (https://ajeworld.com.au/).

These are the initial areas we’re looking to prioritise & I'd love to discuss the brief with you in more detail. It would be great to get your perspective on which elements you think would require UX input versus those that are more straightforward from a development perspective and could be scoped without UX support.

Let me know a good time to connect and I can run you through everything - I can do Friday at 11am if that suits?

Thanks,
Laura

attributes.title: Collection Grid
attributes.description:
In Scope:

  • Product Card grid that displays a filtered list of products and links to their respective PDPs

Out of scope:

  • Alternative Collection/Category layouts

Acceptance criteria:

  • Products display in grid format correctly

  • Pagination correctly updates results

  • Grid is responsive across breakpoints

References:

  • https://www.figma.com/design/DxOY3rxIdGfjgrirLYyxjg/Jardan---Website-Redesign--Master-?node-id=12559-336349&t=lItbnZYGftm9fpFy-0

attributes.title: Filter and Sort
attributes.description:
In Scope:

  • Filter bar controlling the results displayed below:

    • Results count

    • Range filters:

      • This should be based on collection attributes e.g. find all of the 'Range' metafield values for the collections selected for this template and display them as filter options

      • Clicking these buttons will filter the results shown and replace the grid below with a standard product card grid

    • Product/Gallery view toggle

      • Sort option is shown in place of this for specified collections

Out of scope:

  • Product grid layout - this element will control what is displayed there, but the actual contents are handled in another task

Acceptance criteria:

  • Results count updates correctly

  • Filters work as expected and update results

  • Layout toggle options work correctly

  • Sort option correctly reorders results

References:

  • https://www.figma.com/design/DxOY3rxIdGfjgrirLYyxjg/Jardan---Website-Redesign--Master-?node-id=11649-24331&p=f&t=nwfdbgUZPOGqJqnV-0

attributes.title: Handee Blog
attributes.description:
While adding blogs to the Handee Shopify site, I discovered that blog posts aren't visible live or in previews, even when published and set to visible.

 

For example, this old post I found - which is set to 'visible' (https://handee.co.nz/blogs/news/essity-invests-20-million-to-decarbonise-kawerau-paper-production) redirects to the homepage (/).

The attached indicates that the redirect code set in the theme's blog templates.

 

Could you please check this and remove the redirects and make the blog page visible, after which I can test the blog index (/blogs/news) and individual posts.

attributes.title: Text
attributes.description:
In Scope:

  • Rich text body block for use within article/page body

  • Supports: paragraphs, bold, italic, links, unordered lists

  • Optional sub-heading (smaller than main heading, larger than body)

  • Configurable max-width (e.g. full-width or constrained column)

Out of scope:

  • Multi-column text layout within a single block - use multiple blocks side by side

  • Embedded media (images, videos) within the text block - use dedicated media blocks

  • Table formatting

  • Custom font size or colour overrides per paragraph

Acceptance criteria:

  • Rich text renders all standard formatting options correctly

  • Sub-heading renders with distinct typographic styling

  • Line length is readable (max ~70ch for body text)

  • Links styled per the design system (bullet CTA style or inline underline)

References:

  • https://www.figma.com/design/DxOY3rxIdGfjgrirLYyxjg/Jardan---Website-Redesign--Master-?node-id=11649-25242&m=dev

attributes.title: NPFG US Site Stat Review
attributes.description:
Hi Kate, see below the email from Shannon

Summary:
The US site has recently launched and is currently stabilising. Initial reporting in Shopify shows lower than expected sessions and conversion rate compared to pre-launch benchmarks.

Context:

  • Previous benchmarks:

    • K9: ~1.5K visitors per day

    • Feline: ~2.5K–3K visitors per day

  • Expected combined traffic should be higher than current Shopify reporting

  • Conversion rate is also lower than anticipated

  • Client noted ~70% of sales are from repeat customers

Request:

  • Validate Shopify session tracking and conversion rate accuracy

  • Compare pre vs post launch performance across key metrics

  • Identify any potential gaps in tracking, attribution, or data capture

  • Assess if current performance could be normal post-launch volatility or if further investigation is required

attributes.title: Room Planner
attributes.description:
Overview

Design the user experience for Jardan's Room Planner within the Design Studio- a tool that allows customers and trade clients to plan room layouts with Jardan furniture, rugs, and, in the future, homewares. The planner sits within Epicor CPQ and supports checkout as individual line items.

The existing made-to-order PDP has already been designed. This scope covers the Room Planner experience and the amendments needed to connect the PDP to the Room Planner handoff.

Platforms required

  • Desktop — primary design target

  • Mobile — responsive designs

  • Tablet — key device for trade showroom use

—

Design Studio run-through meeting notes:

Design Studio Current Functionality

  • Room planner with checkout capability for furniture, rugs, and eventually homewares

  • Built using nested configurators within EpiCor CPQ (functionality only available since January 2026)

  • Each product becomes individual line item when sent to Kinetic

  • Core features:

    • 3D/2D view toggle with zoom controls

    • Rotate function (45-degree increments only due to KDMax limitations)

    • Copy function for modular pieces

    • Delete individual pieces

    • Room dimensions input capability

    • Save room configurations with positions and customizations

Product Placement & Collision Management

  • Current system places new items at zero coordinates (overlapping issue)

  • Proposed solution: 2D grid-based placement system similar to Rhoda Online

    • User selects piece from sidebar

    • Clicks desired position on 2D grid

    • Eliminates automatic collision detection complexity

  • No collision rules planned due to:

    • Lack of detailed furniture dimension data

    • Complexity of valid overlaps (chairs under tables, etc.)

Modular Furniture Configuration

  • Nested configurator structure: Design Studio → Product → Modular pieces

  • Apply fabric changes to all modular pieces simultaneously via parent-level controls

  • Customers frequently purchase modular pieces in different materials

  • EpiCor confirmation pending on multi-layer inheritance capability

  • May require fallback to flat structure if nested approach unavailable

Integration & User Journey

  • PDP to Design Studio handoff required for multi-product rooms

  • Cannot add new pieces within standalone configurators

  • Pending confirmation: Can Design Studio preload products/customizations from PDP

  • Primary use cases:

    • Trade customers in showrooms using tablets

    • B2B client consultations

    • Room planning tool for sales teams

Additional Product Requirements

  • Custom rugs: Variable length/width, fixed height, material selection only

  • Sustainability options: Checkbox for sustainably sourced timber (details pending meeting with Teresa)

  • New outdoor range: Sofa with wooden frame and attached marble side table

  • In-stock vs made-to-order rug categories

attributes.title: Projects Page Update
attributes.description:
Aim: Our projects are key selling tools for our business. The aim of this redesign is to ensure that our projects are visible sitewide, clients are inspired by the selection and variety of projects and can easily find information they need. 

We would like your recommendations on how to:

  1. Display our projects - ie projects page look & feel and functionality 

  2. Integrate the projects across the site so clients are inspired throughout 

  3. Consider how this relates to More Space. It is currently part of more space - please consider the role overall of content across the site. 

Update should be:

  • More inspiring 

  • Easy to navigate 

  • Easy to filter between residential or commercial  

We have three categories of projects for our content. They are separated by the content we have access to, and the time we have to turn them around to be online, and how in depth we cover them. 

  1. Level 1 | 1-2 Images | Simple Caption - Project, Designer/Architect/ Featured Products
    Fast and easy to load in house  

  2. Level 2 | Multi Images | Copy – 1-2 Paragraphs (+if appropriate a quote) |Caption - Project, Designer/Architect/ Featured Products
    Short and sharp overview of project – copywriter needed with short turnaround  

  3. Level 3 | Multi Images | Copy – Story / Interview |Caption - Project, Designer/Architect/ Featured Products 
    More in depth – longer lead time  
     

Suggested Features 

  1. Filter – Client should be able to filter by Type – Residential or Commercial with relevant sub-categories. For commercial this would include public spaces / Hospitality / Education / Workplace .

  2. Hyperlinking functionality to “read more” and click through to specific project page if the content has been built and available. 

  3. Hover function or underneath project name.

  4. Explore option to also show on PDP page - for example tagged images with Tufty 

 

Design

Please suggest format - is grid the best option? 

  • Consider Portrait & Landscape Images

  • We like the cascade / editorial feel of Zimmerman and or simple grid of Mobilia

  • Level 2 and 3 above need to link to their individual project page.

  • Project pages will vary depending on images and copy available.

  • Examples of level 2 landing page are as follows;  

    • Iconic furniture and hybrid thinking at 120 Collins Street | Space Furniture 

    • Space Atelier's new interiors for Palma by Graya | Space Furniture

attributes.title: [Performance & Strategy] Ongoing UX Optimisations (Monthly Cadence)
attributes.description:
Outcome: Monthly UX program — proactively reviews site performance, surfaces revenue-aligned quick wins, recommends tests where useful.

Context (21 Apr call):

- Delivered by DC UX team — separate from QBR (which is delivered by business consultant).

- Suggested approach: focus on quick wins first, layer in A/B testing later. Hairhouse cautious about A/B testing because of long internal dev pipeline and concern that tests will sit unimplemented for months.

- Hairhouse eCom Coordinator already runs site conversion review work — fold into same bucket.

- Search & merch is flagged as the team's biggest expected conversion lever.

Open questions:

- Confirm cadence (monthly vs other).

- Confirm reporting tool to surface impact (no testing tool decided yet).

Next step: Begin to estimate as next steps.

Outcome: Lift base conversion in static (non-promo) weeks via quick wins now; introduce A/B / multivariate testing once dev pipeline allows.

  • Hairhouse has hesitated on a CRO platform because of a 2-yr internal dev backlog — fear that tests sit unimplemented and have to be re-run.

  • Suggested approach: start with quick wins (clearly losing UX patterns — search & merch, error UX, etc.), introduce A/B testing after a couple of months once value is proven.

  • Past tests required further dev to implement winners — slow loop.

  • Tools previously evaluated: A/B Tasty, Optimizely.

  • Hairhouse to compile a hypothesis list (none exists internally yet). Note: Formalise A/B & multivariate testing once dev capacity allows. Next step: Hairhouse compiles hypothesis list → DC reviews + recommends platform shortlist when ready to move.

attributes.title: Make the app more useful through bookings, loyalty, and richer experience
attributes.description:

- 76k downloads, ~15% of online today.

- Strategy: campaigns to drive downloads + exclusive in-app offers + unique app-only features.

- Closely linked to the Bookings ticket - in-app booking management is one of the planned differentiators.

Needs: start off with a kick off meeting to understand requriments

attributes.title: Meeting with designer: Review and update Design System with shared component limitations for SABA & Sportscraft
attributes.description:
Hey Hannah - Can you please book in a meeting with Alex and a designer to run through the customisations, Shopify limitations and app limitations ready to hand back to APG?

Then the designer can update Figma, ready to hand back to APG.
